problem: A row of metric values in the PDF wraps or breaks at different heights across sibling cells, producing visible vertical misalignment a human reader would notice but no text-based check can detect.
evidence: Startkorpus item from issue #511 ("Umbruch und vertikale Fehlausrichtung in Metrikstreifen"). Issue #510's own acceptance criteria names one concrete historical case of this class: the Desktop/Mobile performance comparison used to render as a flat "Desktop 85 · Mobile 67" text line (a single-column metric strip), since fixed by switching to two side-by-side gauge components (src/output/pdf/detail_modules/performance.rs::score_gauge_grid). #510 was closed with a structural (same-run, no stored pixel baseline) regression guard for that specific case rather than a general pixel-diff pipeline: a stored-baseline approach was deliberately rejected because font hinting/anti-aliasing differs across machines/CI runners, making an exact-baseline comparison flaky by construction.
expected: The specific historical case (dual-viewport gauge grid) stays guarded structurally. A general-purpose pixel-diff-against-stored-baseline pipeline for *arbitrary* future metric strips remains undesigned and is not planned unless a concrete new case demonstrates the structural-guard approach doesn't generalize.
regression: src/output/pdf/tests.rs::test_dual_viewport_performance_renders_two_gauges_not_a_flat_strip (covers only the named dual-viewport case, not metric strips in general).
counter_examples: A metric strip where all cells wrap to the same number of lines (even if each individually wraps) is correctly-aligned and must not be flagged by any future generalization of this check.
